factors.summary: Demand appears to be stable with growth in specific segments.

evidence.text: Revenues for the Divergent Solutions segment...new programs previously won in fiscal 2022. Also the year-to-date period benefited from incremental revenues from the StreetLight acquisition.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is stable with growth in specific segments, indicating a positive trend.

factors.summary: Backlog is increasing, with new business awards driving growth.

evidence.text: The increase in backlog in Critical Mission Solutions...offset slower growth in the U.S. Defense market. Backlog levels in People & Places Solutions and Divergent Solutions remained consistent year-over-year.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing backlog with new business awards suggests strong demand and growth opportunities.

factors.summary: Acquisitions are contributing positively, with successful integration.

evidence.text: The year-to-date period benefited from incremental revenues from the StreetLight acquisition...unfavorably impacted by $71.7 million for our international businesses.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Acquisitions are contributing positively, showing effective capital allocation and integration success.

factors.summary: The company is focused on retaining key employees amidst separation plans and other challenges.

evidence.text: the possibility that we may not retain key employees while the separation is pending or after it is completed, as well as factors related to our business, such as our ability to fully execute on our three-year corporate strategy, including our ability to invest in the tools needed to implement our strategy, competition from existing and future competitors in our target markets, our ability to achieve the cost-savings and synergies contemplated by our recent acquisitions within the expected time frames or to achieve them fully and to successfully integrate acquired businesses while retaining key personnel.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Focus on retaining employees amidst challenges, but no clear indication of success or failure.

factors.summary: The company is launching new products and services in sustainable and digital transformation areas.

evidence.text: As our clients navigate the digital transformation and growing cyber risks, we have positioned ourselves at the forefront of this growth, adding digital capabilities, products and tools to serve a growing set of customers.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Launching new products in growing areas indicates innovation and strategic focus.

factors.summary: The company is focusing on climate response and sustainability in its operations and services.

evidence.text: As a purpose-led company, we know we have a pivotal role to play in addressing the climate emergency in collaboration with our clients, our employees and our entire stakeholder base. We consider this not only good business, but our duty to channel our technology-enabled expertise and capabilities toward benefiting people and the planet.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Focus on sustainability and climate response shows proactive corporate responsibility.

factors.summary: The consulting and advisory segment is expanding through strategic partnerships and market focus.

evidence.text: Together with our visionary partner, PA Consulting, we're establishing our position in high-end advisory services, creating a springboard to expand in high value offerings beyond the core.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Expanding through partnerships and market focus indicates growth and strategic advancement.

factors.summary: Critical Mission Solutions (CMS) is being separated, signaling strategic focus on core competencies.

evidence.text: On May 9, 2023, the Company announced its intention to separate its CMS business, resulting in two independent publicly traded companies. Jacobs expects to complete the separation in fiscal 2024 through a distribution to Jacobs' stockholders that is intended to be tax-free to Jacobs’ stockholders for U.S. federal income tax purposes.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Separation of CMS may indicate strategic focus but has mixed implications for the company's future.

factors.summary: People & Places Solutions (P&PS) is addressing climate change and energy transition with end-to-end solutions.

evidence.text: Jacobs' People & Places Solutions line of business provides end-to-end solutions for our clients’ most complex challenges related to climate change, energy transition, connected mobility, integrated water management, smart cities and biopharmaceutical manufacturing.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The factor highlights successful end-to-end solutions addressing significant global challenges like climate change and energy transition.

factors.summary: Divergent Solutions is enhancing digital and cyber capabilities to support core segments.

evidence.text: Jacobs’ new operating segment, Divergent Solutions, serves as the core foundation for developing and delivering innovative, next-generation cloud, cyber, data and digital technologies.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The new segment is developing innovative cloud, cyber, and digital technologies, indicating growth and modernization.

factors.summary: PA Consulting is expanding its digital transformation and sustainability projects, enhancing client impact.

evidence.text: PA Consulting also expanded its work with patented sustainable product and packaging solution, PulPac, with the Blister Packs Collective – an initiative whose aim is to reduce and eliminate the use of PVC and other plastics in tablet packs; and led leading offshore wind developer, Corio Generation, to secure a fixed-price electricity contract in Ireland’s first offshore wind auction, further establishing it in the renewables space.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Expansion into sustainability and digital transformation projects suggests growth and positive impact on clients.

factors.summary: Revenue and operating profit have shown growth in the three and nine months ended June 30, 2023 compared to the same periods in 2022.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue and operating profit have shown consistent growth, indicating strong performance.

factors.summary: The Critical Mission Solutions segment is experiencing strong growth, particularly in nuclear remediation and U.S. government markets.

evidence.text: Revenue for the three and nine months ended June 30, 2023 was $1.19 billion and $3.46 billion, respectively, an increase of $81.8 million and $236.9 million, or 7.4% and 7.4%, from $1.11 billion and $3.22 billion for the corresponding periods last year.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong growth in key segments like nuclear remediation and government markets supports positive momentum.

factors.summary: Input costs are not explicitly mentioned as rising or stable; however, higher net interest expense and unfavorable currency exchange impacts are affecting results.

evidence.text: Net interest expense for the three and nine months ended June 30, 2023 was $36.0 million and $106.0 million, respectively, an increase of $10.9 million and $41.4 million from $25.1 million and $64.6 million, or 43.3% and 64.0%, for the corresponding periods last year.
evidence.subsection: Overview – Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2023

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: While net interest expense has increased, there's no explicit mention of rising input costs, so the sentiment is mixed.

factors.summary: Labor costs are being managed through restructuring and workforce reductions, particularly in the PA Consulting segment.

evidence.text: During third quarter fiscal 2023, the Company approved a plan to improve business processes and cost structure of our PA Consulting investment by reorganizing senior management and reducing headcount. In connection with these initiatives, the Company incurred approximately $17 million in pre-tax charges during the three and nine month periods ended June 30, 2023, which are expected to be settled in cash by the end of the fiscal year.
evidence.subsection: Overview – Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2023

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring and workforce reductions indicate cost-cutting measures, which may signal challenges or inefficiencies.

factors.summary: Productivity improvements are expected from restructuring initiatives, particularly in the PA Consulting segment, with estimated annualized savings.

evidence.text: These activities, which are expected to be substantially completed before the end of fiscal 2023, are expected to result in estimated gross annualized pre-tax cash savings of approximately $40 million to $50 million.
evidence.subsection: Overview – Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2023

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Expected productivity improvements and annualized savings suggest positive developments from restructuring.

factors.summary: Market share in the Critical Mission Solutions segment is improving, driven by new contracts and growth in key markets.

evidence.text: Revenue for the three and nine months ended June 30, 2023 was $1.19 billion and $3.46 billion, respectively, an increase of $81.8 million and $236.9 million, or 7.4% and 7.4%, from $1.11 billion and $3.22 billion for the corresponding periods last year.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Market share is improving due to new contracts and growth in key markets.

factors.summary: Customer diversification is improving as revenue growth is spread across multiple segments and geographies.

evidence.text: Revenue increases for the year over year periods were due mainly to the Company's P&PS and CMS legacy businesses and in addition, to a smaller degree, other increases in our DVS and PA Consulting businesses.
evidence.subsection: Overview – Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2023

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Customer diversification is improving, reducing reliance on a few customers.

factors.summary: Management has provided forward-looking statements indicating expectations for future growth and results, particularly regarding the separation of the CMS business. The company has announced intentions to separate CMS into an independent publicly traded company, signaling confidence in its strategic direction.

evidence.text: Examples of forward-looking statements include... the perceived benefits for both Jacobs and CMS to be derived from the separation.

On May 9, 2023, the Company announced its intention to separate its CMS business into an independent publicly traded company. Jacobs is targeting to complete the separation in fiscal 2024 through a distribution to Jacobs' stockholders that is intended to be tax-free to Jacobs’ shareholders for U.S. federal income tax purposes.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Separation of CMS into an independent company shows strategic confidence and growth expectations.

factors.summary: The company is implementing cost reduction measures, including restructuring and technology investments, while also managing compensation expenses.

evidence.text: The increase for the three month period ended June 30, 2023 was primarily driven by continued higher investments in company technology platforms and higher incentive and other compensation charges.

During third quarter fiscal 2023, the Company approved a plan to improve business processes and cost structure of our PA Consulting investment by reorganizing senior management and reducing headcount. In connection with these initiatives, the Company incurred approximately $17 million in pre-tax charges during the three and nine month periods ended June 30, 2023, which are expected to be settled in cash by the end of the fiscal year.
evidence.subsection: Overview – Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2023

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction measures are being implemented, but they come with associated charges.

factors.summary: The margin trajectory shows mixed performance, with improvements in operating margins in the Divergent Solutions segment but pressure on gross and operating margins due to restructuring costs and unfavorable currency impacts.

evidence.text: Operating profit for the segment was $20.8 million and $57.6 million...increase of 72.0% and 10.3% from the prior year periods.

Gross profit for the three and nine months ended June 30, 2023 was $856.7 million, an increase of $32.3 million, or 3.9%, from $824.5 million from the corresponding period last year and reflecting related gross profit margins of 20.5% and 21.5% for the respective periods.
evidence.subsection: Overview – Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2023

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed performance with some improvements but also pressures on margins.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing organic growth across multiple segments, with particular acceleration in the P&PS and CMS legacy businesses. Growth is also evident in the PA Consulting segment, driven by its Defence & Security, Public Sector, and Energy & Utilities businesses. Additionally, the company is focusing on technology-forward solutions and sustainable markets to further strengthen its competitive advantage.

evidence.text: Revenues for the PA Consulting segment...due to growth in PA Consulting's Defence & Security, Public Sector and Energy & Utilities businesses.
By shaping our future, we will produce outsized results. [...] This puts us in a unique position to further strengthen our competitive advantage across our core sectors.

Revenue increases for the year over year periods were due mainly to the Company's P&PS and CMS legacy businesses and in addition, to a smaller degree, other increases in our DVS and PA Consulting businesses.
evidence.subsection: Overview – Three and Nine Months Ended June 30, 2023

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong organic growth across multiple segments and focus on technology solutions.
